label: small_kernel_second_pass_after_direct_selected_moe
scope: Reopened Task #4 RMSNorm/router/GDN small-kernel decode overhead after router256 and direct-selected-MoE were retained. Requirement: retain only if acceptable for both Q4_K_M and Q4_K_S.
final_source_state: No source changes retained in this second small-kernel pass; working tree is clean except unrelated untracked uv.lock.

probes.name: decode_rmsnorm_threads_128
probes.change: Temporarily passed threads=128 to rows=1 attention RMSNorm, rows==1 post-attention add_rmsnorm, and final output RMSNorm calls instead of the 256-thread wrapper default.
probes.validation: PYTHONPATH=. uv run pytest tests/test_qwen35_gguf_decode_graph_policy.py tests/test_qwen35_gguf_compact_moe_gemv_routing.py tests/test_gguf_ops.py -q => 15 passed
probes.reason: Q4_K_S regressed substantially before the Q4_K_M gate was attempted. The existing 256-thread RMSNorm/add_rmsnorm defaults remain better for this graph-captured decode mix.

probes.name: router_split_shared_coop_threads_128
probes.change: Temporarily retuned the c=1 split-shared cooperative router from the retained 256-thread launch to 128 threads.
probes.validation: Existing routing test intentionally failed until expected thread count would be updated; benchmark was run only as a performance probe.
probes.reason: Q4_K_S did not beat the current direct-selected baseline; retained router256 remains the best tested router launch shape.

probes.name: router_split_shared_coop_threads_64
probes.change: Temporarily retuned the c=1 split-shared cooperative router from 256 threads to 64 threads.
probes.validation: Performance probe only; would require updating the expected routing-thread assertion if retained.
probes.reason: Q4_K_S regressed more than router128; retained router256 remains best.

probes.name: gdn_ssm_out_f32_activation_contract
probes.change: Considered removing the GDN decode f32_to_bf16 cast before ssm_out by routing Q8T16 ssm_out through its existing f32-input single GEMV variant.
probes.validation: Rejected by existing P10.X1 semantic guard tests before benchmarking: tests/test_qwen35_gguf_decode_repack_semantics.py asserts linear-attention ssm_out must not switch to activation_dtype=GGUF_ACTIVATION_F32 because prior work found it was not equivalent enough for MoE routing.
probes.reason: This crosses a known math-contract boundary rather than being a safe launch-reduction. Keeping the BF16 cast preserves the documented decode-repack semantics.

probes.name: decode_graph_steps_per_replay_2
probes.change: No source change: benchmarked --graph-steps-per-replay 2 to reduce host graph launches by replaying two recorded decode steps per launch.
probes.validation: Benchmarks reported finite final logits and stable final token IDs for both Q4_K_M and Q4_K_S.
probes.reason: Q4_K_M was effectively neutral and Q4_K_S gained only +0.11%; changing the runtime/API default from one-step replay to two-step replay would make default replay semantics less flexible for a sub-threshold dual-column result. Keep as an optional benchmark knob.

decision: No additional RMSNorm/router/GDN small-kernel source change retained after router256 and direct-selected-MoE. The retained baseline remains 4K/128 Q4_K_M 99.226 tok/s and Q4_K_S 100.255 tok/s.
